en.extract:
Meek's pygmy parrot (Micropsitta meeki), also known as the yellow-breasted pigmy parrot, is a species of small parrot in the family Psittacidae. It is endemic to Papua New Guinea.


Taxonomy and etymology
This bird is one of seven named in honour of the English naturalist Albert Stewart Meek, who collected many previously unknown birds and insects from Australia, Papua New Guinea, and the Pacific region. Many of these are now in the Natural History Museum in London.
The two subspecies are M. m. meeki Rothschild & Hartert, 1914, and M. m. proxima Rothschild & Hartert, 1924.


Description
Pygmy parrots are the smallest members of the true parrots superfamily. They are fast-moving, emerald-green birds with brilliant flashes of colour. Meek's pygmy parrot has a cap, forehead, nape, and face of mid-brown with a yellow throat and underparts. In M. m. meeki, the yellow colouring extends to the ear coverts, while in M. m. proxima, it also extends as a narrowing band of yellow horizontally above the eyes. In the latter subspecies, the face and nape are a paler brown. The remainder of the plumage is green, slightly darker on the back and wings, and paler on the belly. The beak and large feet are brown.


Distribution
Meek's pygmy parrot is known from the Admiralty Islands, a group of eighteen islands in the Bismarck Archipelago to the north of Papua New Guinea. It is said to be common on the islands of Lou and Manus and the population appears to be stable so it is listed as being of "Least Concern" in the IUCN Red List of Threatened Species.


Biology
Meek's pygmy parrots have not been much studied. They spend their day clambering about among the foliage of trees using their beaks, large feet, and stiffened tail feathers for support. Their diet is believed to be insects, fungi, lichens, and mosses. Attempts to keep pygmy parrots in captivity have not met with success. This may be due to the birds suffering from stress or to a lack of understanding of their dietary requirements.

'''Meek's pygmy parrot''' ('''''Micropsitta meeki'''''), also known as the '''yellow-breasted pigmy parrot''', is a species of small [[parrot]] in the family [[Psittacidae]]. It is [[endemic]] to [[Papua New Guinea]].

==Taxonomy and etymology==
This bird is one of seven named in honour of the English naturalist [[Albert Stewart Meek]], who collected many previously unknown birds and insects from Australia, Papua New Guinea, and the Pacific region.<ref name=Rothschild/> Many of these are now in the [[Natural History Museum, London|Natural History Museum]] in London.<ref name=Rothschild>{{cite web|url=http://www.andrewisles.com/AndrewIsles/pdf/Cat39Web.pdf |title=Rothschild, Walter, Hartert, Ernst and K. Jordan |work=Catalogue 39 |publisher=Andrew Isles |page=36 |access-date=23 January 2013 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070928221410/http://www.andrewisles.com/AndrewIsles/pdf/Cat39Web.pdf |archive-date=28 September 2007 }}</ref>

The two subspecies are ''M. m. meeki'' Rothschild & Hartert, 1914, and ''M. m. proxima'' Rothschild & Hartert, 1924.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.zoonomen.net/avtax/psit.html |title=Psittaciformes: Micropsittinae |author=Alan P. Peterson |date=16 May 2012 |work=Zoological Nomenclature Resource |access-date=23 January 2013}}</ref>

==Description==
Pygmy parrots are the smallest members of the [[Psittacoidea|true parrots]] superfamily. They are fast-moving, emerald-green birds with brilliant flashes of colour.<ref name=AvianWeb/> Meek's pygmy parrot has a cap, forehead, nape, and face of mid-brown with a yellow throat and underparts. In ''M. m. meeki'', the yellow colouring extends to the ear coverts, while in ''M. m. proxima'', it also extends as a narrowing band of yellow horizontally above the eyes. In the latter subspecies, the face and nape are a paler brown. The remainder of the plumage is green, slightly darker on the back and wings, and paler on the belly. The beak and large feet are brown.<ref>{{cite book |title=[[Handbook of the Birds of the World]]. Volume 4: Sandgrouse to Cuckoos |editor-last=del Hoyo |author=Josep |editor-last2=Elliot |author2=Andrew | editor-last3=Sargatal | editor-first3=Jordi|year=1997 |publisher=Lynx Edicions |isbn=84-87334-22-9 }}</ref>

==Distribution==
Meek's pygmy parrot is known from the [[Admiralty Islands]], a group of eighteen islands in the [[Bismarck Archipelago]] to the north of Papua New Guinea.<ref name=AvianWeb>{{cite web |url=http://www.beautyofbirds.com/pygmyparrots.htm |title=Meek's Pygmy Parrots |work=Pygmy Parrots |publisher=Avian World |access-date=23 January 2013}}</ref> It is said to be common on the islands of Lou and Manus and the population appears to be stable so it is listed as being of "[[Least Concern]]" in the [[IUCN Red List of Threatened Species]].<ref name="iucn status" />

==Biology==
Meek's pygmy parrots have not been much studied. They spend their day clambering about among the foliage of trees using their beaks, large feet, and stiffened tail feathers for support. Their diet is believed to be [[insect]]s, [[fungi]], [[lichen]]s, and [[moss]]es.<ref name=AvianWeb/> Attempts to keep pygmy parrots in captivity have not met with success. This may be due to the birds suffering from stress or to a lack of understanding of their dietary requirements.<ref name=AvianWeb/>
